pages.full_text:
We stopped at El Astral at Tordesillas nr to Valladolid The storm came close so the boys weren't allowed to swim at first. Then we all swam, except Simon, who wasn't feeling & very well after all yesterday's fruit & gas pacho. [marginalia: bisomething of] (campersham to h. Auvero again like [?Portugal])
So [marginalia: Friday 10th Aug] [marginalia: breakfast something] we left at 10.35. Simon's better.
The loos don't seem as clean as France. Dirty water left around in mop buckets (not at El Astral).
ch Broken things. Men don't mind. Broken brackets & screws on children's playgrounds. 2 out of 3 lav. do locks broken. Shower taps fall off. Home-made or badly designed shower cubicles. Not enough hooks.
A We stopped for lunch, + quickly moved on because of 8 wasps arriving in 2 minutes. We next stopped in a field of poplars near Lerma. Fewer wasps.
A disappointing afternoon. 2 church visits, + we couldn't get in. The first, 3.15 Covar Rubias, was a church which was shut until 4.30. The village was pretty, a __monastery__ is there, but we drove on.
The second had a plain church which we saw, + a beautiful cloister, but the queue was too long. San Domingo de Silos, a
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible on this page. Marginalia is dense and partially illegible — 'bisomething of', 'breakfast something', and a word like 'campersham/campershan' with a reference to 'h. Auvero again like [?Portugal]'. 'Covar Rubias' may be 'Covarrubias' (a known village near Burgos, Spain). 'San Domingo de Silos' is likely 'Santo Domingo de Silos'. Year estimated as 1990 based on volume position and Spain/France travel context, but cannot be confirmed from this page alone.

pages.full_text:
monastery is still there.
We carried on to El Frontal [?++++] at Abegar. Expensive, a lovely clover-shaped swimming pool + a [strikethrough: con] canopy of pines. Mini football. There is a lake, but we haven't been there.
Sat 11th Aug
We have spent a lazy day at the pool. Very pleasant. OK. lunch in camp restaurant, but horrible rock music.
Simon cooked delicious tom. + egg fry-up for supper, + next door campers blared out more rock music at full volume until Simon + Thomas asked them to switch it off. Nice not having any dau.
Sun 12th Aug
We left at 10.30. Bought petrol. As we got to Molinos to go to church, everyone was filing out. It was an upstairs room. Too bad, it looked merry with lots of children. On to Vinuesa, a nice church, candlelit. Balconies in the village. Boys playing tennis on half a court + wall. Wash house, long + low, with river, mill-race, flowing through. Stones for women to kneel + rub clothes. Paddock at the back. We went over a very high pass, [?5] 1 nes. On to where there was a good restaurant, lot
pages.notes: Page number 79 visible top right. 'El Frontal' campsite continues from left page. 'Abegar' may be 'Abejar' (village in Soria province, Spain). After 'El Frontal' there appear to be asterisks or symbols followed by a word that could be 'woody'. The text ends mid-sentence at the bottom. '[?5] 1 nes' is unclear — could be a distance like '51 kms' or similar. 'Nice not having any dau.' — 'dau.' may be abbreviation for 'daughters' or similar. Year estimated as 1990 from volume position and Spain itinerary context.
